title: apoballis mutata
intro: Apoballis mutata, also known as the 'Taro Vine', is a perennial climber with edible tubers that belongs to the family Arum. The tubers of the Apoballis mutata plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming the tubers of Apoballis mutata include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the tubers are typically dried, powdered, or boiled to extract their active compounds, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or supplements.
